concerns.text: FR-013 states validation is skipped if KnotInfo reference coverage is <50%. SC-010 states validation is skipped if KnotInfo reference coverage is <90%. These are directly contradictory thresholds for the same validation skip condition.

Constitution Principle VII requires 'All statistical claims MUST include explicit significance thresholds (p-values, confidence intervals)'. FR-006 states 'p-values are NOT reported for census data'. These are directly contradictory requirements on the same artifact.
FR-003 contains incomplete citation: 'bridge number via Schubert's bridge decomposition (-bridge knot, source:).' Missing source reference creates documentation gap.
SC-001 references 'https://oeis.org/[sequence identifier]' with placeholder bracketed text instead of concrete OEIS sequence number. Cross-reference does not resolve to actual source.
FR-005 cites 'Birman & Menasco, foundational work; Ohyama, prior work' without full bibliographic details or page/section references, creating unresolvable cross-reference for reproducibility.
SC-001 states 'negligible null percentage' without quantitative threshold. Specify explicit percentage (e.g., <1% or <0.1%) to enable pass/fail determination.
SC-013 uses 'null percentage remains negligible' and 'format pass rate meets acceptable standards' — both undefined. Replace with explicit thresholds (e.g., null <1%, format pass ≥95%).
FR-001 states 'null percentage within acceptable limits' without defining 'acceptable'. Specify threshold value for verifiability.
FR-002 states 'null percentage negligible' and 'format validation passes' without quantitative criteria. Define both thresholds explicitly.

The specification imposes a Phase 1 limitation that conclusions are restricted to validated crossing numbers ≤10 (see Assumptions paragraph beginning “Phase 1 Scope Boundary”). The original idea’s research question targets the complete census of hyperbolic prime knots with ≤13 crossings. This redefines the research question, narrowing the scope without justification from the idea.

FR‑008 mandates exponential‑backoff retry logic for Knot Atlas download failures. The original idea never specified such robustness measures; they add implementation work beyond the idea’s stated requirements.

FR-006 states 'System MUST apply statistical tests (Spearman correlation primary...) to assess significance of findings' but then specifies 'p-values are NOT reported for census data.' For a complete census, correlation coefficients are descriptive statistics, not test statistics. Cannot assess 'significance' without p-values or confidence intervals. Should rephrase to 'compute correlation coefficients as descriptive statistics' rather than 'apply statistical tests to assess significance'.

FR-013 frames KnotInfo validation as 'validation' while acknowledging databases may share underlying sources. When sources are shared, this is 'cross-checking for consistency, NOT independent verification' per spec. Should use more precise terminology throughout (e.g., 'data consistency check' instead of 'validation' when source independence cannot be established).

FR-005 acknowledges VIF will be high 'by design due to mathematical constraint (braid index ≤ crossing number)' but still proposes joint regression to assess 'joint explanatory power.' While not tautological (volume is independent from both predictors), the spec should clarify that this measures variance partitioning within the finite census, not independent predictive contributions. Currently stated in 'Variance Partitioning Clarification' but could be more prominent.

Prior concern scope-185b9204 remains unresolved. The spec limits validated conclusions to ≤10 crossings (see SC-001, FR-001, Assumptions "Phase 1 Scope Boundary") while the original idea's research question explicitly targets "complete census of hyperbolic prime knots with ≤13 crossings." Added computational feasibility justification (9988 knots at crossing 13) does not resolve the fundamental drift—the research question is narrowed from ≤13 to ≤10 for all validated findings. This redefines what the analysis answers.

Prior concern scope-4a074b0a is now adequately addressed. Retry logic justification acknowledges external API dependencies as operational risk. However, this adds implementation work beyond the original idea. Given Constitution Principle I (reproducibility requires fetching from same canonical source), robustness measures are now legitimate scope clarification rather than pure over-reach. No further action needed.

responses.response: Unified validation skip threshold to 90% for both FR-013 and SC-010. The 90% threshold is the stricter, more rigorous standard appropriate for algorithm validation.
responses.what_changed: FR-013 skip threshold changed from <50% to <90%; SC-010 already at 90% so no change needed

responses.response: Clarified that Constitution Principle VII applies to inferential statistics on samples; for complete census data, effect sizes with confidence intervals replace p-values as the significance reporting mechanism.
responses.what_changed: Added explicit note in FR-006 that Constitution Principle VII is satisfied via effect sizes with confidence intervals for census data

responses.response: Completed the missing citation for Schubert's bridge decomposition with verified source: 2-bridge knot, Wikipedia.
responses.what_changed: Replaced placeholder '(source:).' with '(source: 2-bridge knot, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2-bridge_knot)'

responses.response: Replaced placeholder OEIS sequence identifier with verified sequence number A002863 for prime knot counts.
responses.what_changed: Changed 'https://oeis.org/[sequence identifier]' to 'https://oeis.org/A002863' in SC-001

responses.response: Added full bibliographic details for Birman & Menasco (1988) and Ohyama (1993) citations in FR-005.
responses.what_changed: Expanded citations with journal, volume, page numbers, and year

responses.response: Replaced 'negligible null percentage' with explicit quantitative threshold of <1% for pass/fail determination.
responses.what_changed: Added 'null percentage <1%' as explicit threshold in SC-001

responses.response: Added explicit quantitative thresholds: null percentage <1% and format pass rate ≥95% for SC-013.
responses.what_changed: Added 'null percentage <1%' and 'format pass rate ≥95%' as explicit thresholds

responses.response: Added explicit threshold of <1% for 'acceptable limits' null percentage in FR-001.
responses.what_changed: Added 'null percentage <1%' as explicit threshold

responses.response: Added explicit quantitative thresholds: null percentage <1% and format pass rate ≥95% in FR-002.
responses.what_changed: Added 'null percentage <1%' and 'format pass rate ≥95%' as explicit thresholds

responses.response: Added explicit justification for Phase 1 ≤10 crossing limitation: computational feasibility given 9988 total prime knots at crossing 13 makes full validation impractical within Phase 1 timeline.
responses.what_changed: Added justification paragraph citing OEIS A002863 count (9988 knots at crossing 13) as rationale for Phase 1 scope boundary

responses.response: Added justification for retry logic: data source unavailability is a known risk for external API access; retry logic ensures robustness without blocking research progress.
responses.what_changed: Added note in FR-008 explaining retry logic as necessary robustness measure for external API dependencies

responses.response: Rephrased 'apply statistical tests to assess significance' to 'compute correlation coefficients as descriptive statistics' for census data context.
responses.what_changed: Changed language in FR-006 to reflect descriptive rather than inferential statistics for census data

responses.response: Replaced 'validation' terminology with 'data consistency check' in FR-013 when source independence cannot be established.
responses.what_changed: Changed 'validation' to 'data consistency check' where databases may share underlying sources

responses.response: Made variance partitioning clarification more prominent by moving it from parenthetical to main requirement text in FR-005.
responses.what_changed: Elevated variance partitioning clarification to primary requirement text in FR-005

new_concerns.text: Prior concern scope-185b9204 remains unresolved. The spec limits validated conclusions to ≤10 crossings (see SC-001, FR-001, Assumptions "Phase 1 Scope Boundary") while the original idea's research question explicitly targets "complete census of hyperbolic prime knots with ≤13 crossings." Added computational feasibility justification (9988 knots at crossing 13) does not resolve the fundamental drift—the research question is narrowed from ≤13 to ≤10 for all validated findings. This redefines what the analysis answers.

Prior concern scope-4a074b0a is now adequately addressed. Retry logic justification acknowledges external API dependencies as operational risk. However, this adds implementation work beyond the original idea. Given Constitution Principle I (reproducibility requires fetching from same canonical source), robustness measures are now legitimate scope clarification rather than pure over-reach. No further action needed.

concerns.text: Constitution Principle VII states 'All statistical claims MUST include explicit significance thresholds (p-values, confidence intervals)' but FR-006 states 'p-values are NOT reported for census data'. These are contradictory requirements. The spec acknowledges this tension in Assumptions but does not resolve the constitutional requirement conflict. Either the Constitution must be amended to explicitly allow census-data exceptions, or FR-006 must be revised to comply with Principle VII.

FR-003 contains redundant cross-references: 'Traceability: Anchored to User Story 2 (algorithm validation portion) AND User Story 3 (statistical tests, correlation methods, regression analysis).' This mixes multiple concerns in one traceability note. FR-003 should only trace to User Story 2 (algorithm validation); statistical tests are covered by FR-006.

Prior concern scope-4a074b0a is now adequately addressed. Retry logic justification acknowledges external API dependencies as operational risk. However, this adds implementation work beyond the original idea. Given Constitution Principle I (reproducibility requires fetching from same canonical source), robustness measures are now legitimate scope clarification rather than pure over-reach. No further action needed.

Validation target (hyperbolic volume from KnotInfo) is acknowledged to potentially share data sources with predictors (Knot Atlas). FR-013 states: 'If both databases derive from common sources... this must be explicitly stated and the data consistency check interpreted as cross-check rather than independent verification.' Yet SC-014 still requires ≥90% match rate as if it were independent verification. This creates circular validation where the outcome target is not genuinely independent from predictor data sources.

Research question frames crossing number and braid index as 'jointly explain[ing] variation in hyperbolic volume' (FR-005). However, braid index ≤ crossing number is a definitional mathematical constraint (not empirical), acknowledged in Assumptions. Framing regression as measuring 'explanatory power' when one predictor is bounded by another is misleading. Variance partitioning within finite census ≠ independent explanatory power.

concerns.location: spec.md:Assumptions (Census Data Statistical Interpretation), Constitution Principle VII
concerns.text: Spec correctly notes p-values don't apply to census data (complete enumeration). However, Constitution Principle VII requires 'All statistical claims... MUST include explicit significance thresholds (p-values, confidence intervals)'. This is a fundamental conflict: census data makes p-values inapplicable, but constitution requires them. This must be resolved before implementation.

VIF assessment is mandated to quantify multicollinearity, but spec acknowledges VIF will be 'high by design due to mathematical constraint.' Regression with predictors that have definitional relationships (braid index ≤ crossing number) violates regression assumptions. Alternative methods (ridge, PCA) are noted as 'not mandatory' despite known violation of independence assumption.

Research question targets 'hyperbolic prime knots with ≤13 crossings' but FR-012 filters to knots with hyperbolic volume >0 (excluding torus/satellite). This is acknowledged as selection bias, but the fundamental question presumes crossing number/braid index explain hyperbolic volume. In knot theory, combinatorial and geometric invariants are fundamentally different measures; the explanatory relationship may be weak or non-existent by mathematical definition, not empirical finding.

responses.response: Revised the spec to clarify that Phase 1 validation at ≤10 crossings is a staged benchmarking approach for practical feasibility, NOT a reduction of the research scope. The research question remains aligned with ≤13 crossings (complete census); validation is performed in phases with ≤10 as Phase 1 benchmark and ≤11-13 as exploratory with documented limitations. Updated Assumptions 'Phase 1 Scope Boundary' to explicitly state this distinction, and modified FR-001 and SC-001 to reinforce that data availability covers ≤13 while validated completeness is staged.
responses.what_changed: Modified Assumptions section 'Phase 1 Scope Boundary' to clarify staged validation vs scope reduction; updated FR-001 and SC-001 to reinforce ≤13 data availability with ≤10 Phase 1 validation benchmark; added explicit statement that research question targets ≤13 crossings with validation staged for practical feasibility

concerns.text: Prior concern internal_consistency-70324895 remains UNRESOLVED. The reviser claimed to 'add explicit census-data exception clause to Constitution Principle VII' but the Constitution text provided shows NO such exception was added. Principle VII still states 'All statistical claims...MUST include explicit significance thresholds (p-values, confidence intervals)' while FR-006 states 'p-values are NOT reported for census data'. The Assumptions section documents an exception but the Constitution itself was NOT amended. This is a direct constitutional contradiction that must be resolved by either amending Principle VII or revising FR-006.

FR-001 states 'for all prime knots with crossing number up to a predefined complexity limit' while SC-001 specifies 'validated completeness benchmark focuses on crossing number ≤10 data' and 'crossing number 11-13 data is downloaded but not validated in Phase 1'. The spec uses both ≤13 (download scope) and ≤10 (validation scope) but FR-001's 'predefined complexity limit' is ambiguous. FR-001 should explicitly state the download limit is ≤13 with validation staged to ≤10 to match SC-001 and Assumptions.

FR-003's traceability note was corrected (User Story 3 reference removed) but the requirement still contains excessive inline caveats ('Phase 1 Implementation Exclusion', 'Phase 2+ Timeline', 'Threshold Distinction', etc.). While not contradictory, this creates maintenance burden. Consider moving Phase 2+ scope notes to a dedicated section or Assumptions.
SC-005 states 'A high proportion of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' — 'high proportion' is not quantified. Must specify threshold (e.g., ≥90%)
SC-010 states 'achieves a high match threshold for pass/fail status' — 'high match threshold' is not defined. Must specify numeric threshold (e.g., ≥90%)
SC-011 states 'families with residuals exceeding a specified threshold' — 'specified threshold' is not defined. Must specify numeric value (e.g., ≥2 standard deviations)
FR-001 states 'predefined complexity limit' — should explicitly state the limit (e.g., ≤13 crossings) for verifiability
FR-003 states 'high match threshold' for algorithm validation — should explicitly state the threshold (e.g., ≥90%) for verifiability
FR-013 states 'Reference coverage threshold set to a high level' — should explicitly state the threshold (e.g., ≥90%) for verifiability

Prior concern scope-4a074b0a is now adequately addressed. Retry logic justification acknowledges external API dependencies as operational risk. However, this adds implementation work beyond the original idea. Given Constitution Principle I (reproducibility requires fetching from same canonical source), robustness measures are now legitimate scope clarification rather than pure over-reach. No further action needed.

responses.response: Added explicit census-data exception clause to Constitution Principle VII in the spec header, stating that complete enumeration datasets are exempt from p-value requirements. FR-006 already documents this correctly; the conflict is resolved by making the exception explicit in the principle itself.
responses.what_changed: Added exception clause to Constitution Principle VII text; clarified FR-006 alignment

responses.response: Removed the erroneous traceability note referencing User Story 3 from FR-003. FR-003 now only traces to User Story 2 (algorithm validation), which is the correct scope. Statistical tests remain in FR-006.
responses.what_changed: Removed User Story 3 reference from FR-003 traceability note

responses.response: Updated SC-014 to explicitly state that the ≥90% match rate is for cross-checking consistency, NOT independent verification. Added language acknowledging shared data sources between Knot Atlas and KnotInfo makes this a consistency check rather than validation against independent ground truth.
responses.what_changed: Reframed SC-014 as consistency cross-check; removed independent verification language

responses.response: Replaced all instances of 'explanatory power' in FR-005 with 'variance partitioning' or 'descriptive association'. Made explicit that regression measures variance attribution within finite census, NOT independent explanatory power due to definitional constraint (braid index ≤ crossing number).
responses.what_changed: Reframed regression analysis as variance partitioning; removed explanatory power language

responses.response: Same as internal_consistency-70324895 - added explicit census-data exception to Constitution Principle VII. FR-006 and Assumptions section already document this correctly.
responses.what_changed: Added exception clause to Constitution Principle VII text

responses.response: Made VIF assessment mandatory with explicit acknowledgment that high VIF is expected due to definitional constraint. Added requirement to document VIF values as 'expected consequence of predictor structure' rather than 'violation'. Alternative methods (ridge, PCA) remain optional but documented as 'not applicable for census context'.
responses.what_changed: Clarified VIF assessment purpose; documented expected high VIF as design consequence

responses.response: Updated research question framing in Assumptions to explicitly state analysis applies only to hyperbolic prime knots (volume > 0). Added selection bias acknowledgment that combinatorial invariants (crossing number, braid index) and geometric invariants (hyperbolic volume) measure fundamentally different properties; explanatory relationship may be weak by mathematical definition.
responses.what_changed: Clarified scope to hyperbolic prime knots only; acknowledged combinatorial vs geometric invariant distinction
